title: My Top 2 AI Stocks Flying Under the Radar for May 2026
text: Aehr Test Systems manufactures equipment and machines that test AI chips for data centers, electric vehicles, industrial, and other applications, specializing in wafer-level testing technology. Year-to-date return stands at 379%, with a 12-month return of 961%. Stock currently trades at $99.28 per share with a market cap of $3.1 billion, valued at 14 times earnings. The company reported significant contract bookings, including a $41 million contract from a major hyperscaler client, bringing total pipeline bookings to approximately $92 million. Analysts anticipate a 71% revenue increase in fiscal 2027, with earnings projected to swing from a net loss of $0.09 per share to net income of $0.15 per share. The investment thesis suggests Aehr remains attractive due to its substantial contract backlog and relatively modest valuation compared to its growth trajectory.
